item.title: [UPDATED] Akira Ransomware Gang Actively Exploiting SonicWall VPNs for Network Breaches
item.description:
The Akira ransomware group is actively exploiting vulnerabilities in SonicWall SSL VPN devices to gain initial access to corporate networks. By targeting these widely used, internet-facing appliances, the threat actors can establish a foothold, move laterally, exfiltrate sensitive data, and ultimate...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Vulnerability

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ransomware Attacks Surge by 46% as Threat Actors Target Construction and Manufacturing
item.description:
Despite a slight decrease in overall weekly cyber attacks, ransomware activity has surged by 46%, according to a new report from Check Point Research. This indicates a strategic shift by threat actors towards more focused and impactful ransomware campaigns. The construction, business services, and i...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] CISA Orders Urgent Patching After Chinese Hackers Steal F5 Source Code
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Emergency Directive 26-01, ordering federal agencies to take immediate action after F5 disclosed a severe breach by a sophisticated nation-state actor, reportedly linked to China. The attackers maintained access for at least...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Qilin Ransomware Group Adds New Victims to Leak Site
item.description:
The Qilin ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation continues its campaign of double extortion, recently adding new victims to its data leak site. Among the latest targets are U.S.-based electrical equipment manufacturer Beta Dyne and Middlesex Appraisal Associates. According to research from Resecur...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Vietnam Airlines Breach: 7.3M Customer Records Exposed in Salesforce Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Vietnam Airlines has suffered a massive data breach exposing the records of 7.3 million unique customers. The attack, revealed on October 11, 2025, is attributed to the 'Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters' hacking group, the same collective behind the recent Qantas breach. The compromise occurred in June 202...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] F5 Breached by Nation-State Actor; BIG-IP Source Code Stolen, CISA Issues Emergency Directive
item.description:
Application security vendor F5 has disclosed a major security breach attributed to a 'highly sophisticated nation-state threat actor.' The attackers maintained long-term access to F5's internal development environments, exfiltrating portions of the BIG-IP source code, information on undisclosed vuln...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Qilin Ransomware Hits Japanese Beer Giant Asahi, Steals 27GB of Data
item.description:
The Russia-based Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack that disrupted operations at Asahi Group Holdings, Japan's largest brewing company. The attack, confirmed by Asahi on October 6, impacted order and shipment systems. On its dark web leak site, the Qilin gang stated ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Living Off the Land: Hackers Abuse Velociraptor DFIR Tool to Deploy Ransomware
item.description:
A suspected China-based threat group, Storm-2603, is weaponizing the legitimate open-source digital forensics and incident response (DFIR) tool, Velociraptor. According to Cisco Talos, the attackers are using an outdated and vulnerable version of the tool (exploiting CVE-2025-6264) to gain persisten...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Cl0p Exploits Oracle EBS Zero-Day in Widespread Extortion Campaign, FBI Issues Emergency Warning
item.description:
A financially motivated threat group, claiming ties to the notorious **[Cl0p](https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0114/)** ransomware gang, has been exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability in **[Oracle E-Business Suite](https://www.oracle.com/applications/ebs/)** (EBS). The flaw, **CVE-2025-61882*...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Cl0p Ransomware Exploits Oracle E-Business Suite Zero-Day in Mass Attack
item.description:
The notorious Cl0p ransomware gang is conducting a widespread extortion campaign by exploiting a critical, unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2025-61882) in Oracle E-Business Suite. The campaign, active since at least August, involves Cl0p breaching vulnerable sy...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Vulnerability, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] SonicWall Breach Escalates: 100% of Cloud Backups Confirmed Stolen
item.description:
Firewall vendor SonicWall has dramatically escalated the severity of a recent data breach, confirming that an investigation found that 100% of customers using its cloud backup service had their firewall configuration files stolen. This admission, made on October 6, 2025, after an investigation with ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Redis Patches Critical "RediShell" RCE Flaw (CVE-2025-49844) in Lua Sandbox
item.description:
Redis has released patches for CVE-2025-49844, a critical use-after-free vulnerability nicknamed "RediShell" by the Wiz researchers who discovered it. The flaw, announced on October 7, 2025, allows an authenticated attacker to escape the Lua sandbox and achieve remote code execution (RCE) on the und...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Salesforce Refuses Ransom After "Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters" Steal Data from 40+ Customers
item.description:
Salesforce has officially refused to pay a ransom demand from the 'Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters'—a reported alliance of Scattered Spider, Lapsus$, and ShinyHunters. The group claims to have stolen nearly one billion records from approximately 40 high-profile Salesforce customers, including Adidas, Cisc...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Cl0p Gang Exploits Oracle EBS Zero-Day in Massive Data Theft Spree
item.description:
The Cl0p ransomware syndicate, also known as Graceful Spider, is actively exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2025-61882, in Oracle's E-Business Suite (EBS). The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, allows for unauthenticated remote code execution and has been used to steal data from n...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Ransomware, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Qilin Ransomware Claims Disruptive Attack on Japanese Beverage Giant Asahi
item.description:
The Russia-based Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a significant cyberattack against Asahi Group Holdings, one of Japan's largest beverage companies. The attack, first disclosed in late September 2025, caused major operational disruptions, forcing the suspension of order and ship...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] UK's NCSC Warns of 'Alarming' Rise in Cyberattacks, Doubling in Past Year
item.description:
The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) revealed in its 2025 annual review that it managed 204 "nationally significant" cyberattacks over the past year, more than double the 89 incidents from the previous year. The agency attributed the alarming surge to increasing threats from state-sponsore...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Clop Exploits Critical Oracle Zero-Day; CISA Issues Emergency Patch Directive
item.description:
Multiple international cybersecurity agencies, including CISA, the UK's NCSC, and Singapore's CSA, have issued urgent warnings about a critical zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2025-61882, in Oracle E-Business Suite. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, is being actively exploited by the Clop ransomw...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' Gang Extorts 39 Salesforce Customers on Leak Site
item.description:
A new cybercriminal collective calling itself 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' has emerged, claiming to be a merger of members from Scattered Spider, Lapsus$, and ShinyHunters. The group launched a dark web data leak site over the weekend of October 4-5, listing 39 major companies, including Cisco, Toyot...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Data Breach, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] CISA Warns of Widespread Flaws in Industrial Control Systems from Major Vendors
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has released a series of advisories warning of numerous vulnerabilities in Industrial Control Systems (ICS) from prominent vendors, including Rockwell Automation, Hitachi Energy, Mitsubishi Electric, and Delta Electronics. These flaws ...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Regulatory
